name: overflow-detection
title: Overflow Detection
description: Detects whether an element's content overflows its container using ResizeObserver. Returns a ref to attach and a reactive boolean — no polling, no manual recalculation.

docs.description: Detects whether an element's content overflows its container using ResizeObserver. Returns a ref to attach and a reactive boolean — no polling, no manual recalculation.

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: Which axis to check for overflow. "horizontal" compares scrollWidth > clientWidth, "vertical" compares scrollHeight > clientHeight, "both" checks either. Defaults to "horizontal".

returns.description: A ref to attach to the observed element, and a boolean that updates reactively when overflow state changes.

properties.name: ref
properties.description: Attach to the element whose overflow you want to detect. Must have constrained dimensions (width/height).

properties.name: isOverflowing
properties.description: True when content exceeds container bounds in the specified direction.

notes.title: Common Use Cases
notes.content: Conditionally showing tooltips on truncated text, toggling expand/collapse buttons, applying visual indicators when content is clipped.

notes.title: Requirement
notes.content: The ref'd element must have constrained dimensions. For horizontal detection, the element needs a width constraint (e.g. truncate, max-w-*, fixed width). For vertical, it needs a height constraint.

content.text: This text will overflow when the container is too narrow to fit it all.

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: Which axis to check for overflow. "horizontal" compares scrollWidth > clientWidth, "vertical" compares scrollHeight > clientHeight, "both" checks either. Defaults to "horizontal".

returns.description: A ref to attach to the observed element, and a boolean that updates reactively when overflow state changes.

properties.description: Attach to the element whose overflow you want to detect. Must have constrained dimensions (width/height).
